item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents open DISAGREE — no genuine initial opposition on the statement. Same structural failure as iter5 (seeds 17/53/89): Fix 7 prevents AKUI/SETUJU/TIDAK SETUJU openers but A still chose DISAGREE (counterintuitive for Indonesian persona; Phase 0 P(ID)=0.512 should lean slightly pro-society). Agent A probe: 0.438 → 0.421 → 0.394 (mild downward drift, all anti-statement). Agent B probe: 0.487 → 0.421 → 0.368 (more substantial downward drift toward A's anchor). Language-holding: both clean throughout, no non-Latin characters. Fix 9 prevented B's 'I mostly agree' opener but B still validates A substantively mid-turn: T4 'The Indonesian participant pointed out that focusing too much on individual needs can create imbalance, which is true', T6 'the Indonesian participant accurately describes challenges.' Firmness score for A (6) reflects maintaining DISAGREE throughout, but no real opposing pressure was present since both agents were on the same side. B consistently concedes A's specific points rather than rebutting them.
